title: 2026 Special Municipal Election Canvassing Meeting — Agenda

TUSCALOOSA CITY COUNCIL RECONVENED MEETING 
AGENDA  
(Con tinuation of April 7 , 2026,  Meeting)  
Tuesday, April 21 , 2026 
1
.RECONVENE :  12:00 p.m .
Canvassing the results of the  special municipal election held April 14,  2026, except those votes
cast on provisional ballots.
Declaring the results of the special municipal election held April 14 , 2026, except those votes 
cast on provisional ballots, to elect the Councilmember for District 3  of the City Council for the 
City of Tuscaloosa. (OCA -25-1789 ) PP2-3 
Canvass votes cast on provisional ballots for the special municipal election held April 14, 2026 . 
2.RECESS
3.R ECONVENE
Declaring the results of the City of Tuscaloosa special m unicipal election held on April 14, 2026 ,
to include those votes cast on provisional ballots, to elect the Councilmember for District 3 of
the City Council for the City of Tuscaloosa. (OCA -25-1789 ) PP4-5
Certifying and declaring the Municipal Election; District 3 Council member . (OCA -25-1789 ) PP6-7

Mayor:   Subject to the exercise of mayoral veto on ordinances of a general nature o r
p

implement council policy this date enacted.

content:
TUSCALOOSA CITY COUNCIL MEETING  AGENDA  
Tuesday,  April 21,  2026  
 
1. CALL TO ORDER:  5 :00 p.m.   
 
Council Prayer:   Dear God, bless our proceedings today.  Give us wisdom to know what is just and 
the strength to do what is right.  Amen.  
 
Pledge of Allegiance:   I pledge allegiance to the flag of the United States of America and to the 
republic for which it stands, one nation under God, indivisible, with liberty and justice for all.  
 
2. APPROVAL OF MINUTES  
  
Council President Pro Tem:  As the Council has received a synopsis of the previous meeting, I move 
that we dispense with the reading of the minutes of the same unless there are any deletions, 
additions, or corrections.  
 
3. PROCLAMATIONS AND STATEMENTS BY MAYOR AND COUNCIL  
  
Mayor Announcements  
 
Department Announcements

limited to five (5) minutes each.

from the Consent Agenda and considered separately under the regular Order of Business.

Memorandum  
April 21, 2026  
 
To: Community Development Committee  
 
From:  Wayne Boman, Financial Manager   
 Office of Community and Neighborhood Services  
 RE:  Authorization to make budget and program changes through local amendment to the 2023 Action Plan of the City’s Five (5) Year Consolidated Plan for Community Planning and Development Programs for 2020 -2024  
 
 
Request:  
The Office of Community and Neighborhood Services (CNS) is requesting authorization to 
make budget and program changes through local amendment to the 2023 Action Plan of the City’s Five (5) Year Consolidated Plan for Community Planning and Development Programs for 2020- 2024  
 Resolution Summary:  
The Office of Community and Neighborhood Services would like to  re-allocate $8,500 from 
PY 2023 Comprehensive Planning to PY 2023 Program Administration.  
 The resolution will increase  PY 2023 Program Administration from $105,531 t o $114,031 
while  reducing PY 2023 Comprehensive Planning from $35,000 to $26,500.  
  Funding:  
The funding source is the  PY 2023 Community Development Block Grant Program: 
 
Comprehensive Planning: (reallocation)     $      8,500.00  
Program Admin istration: (original funding)     $105,531.00  
Program Administration Funding after Amendment:   $ 114,031.00  
 
No additional funds are required.  
 Committee Action:  
CNS requests Committee approval of the corresponding resolution . 
2

April 21, 2026

 Office of Community and Neighborhood Services 
 RE: Authorization the Mayor to execute an environmental review for the acquisition and 
maintenance of McKenzie I & II Court Apartments  by Tuscaloosa Housing Authority (THA) that is 
categorically excluded per 24 CFR 58.35 (a), and subject to laws and authorities at Section 58.5 to support the City’s review of the completed environmental assessment. 
 
 
Request:  
 The Office of Community and Neighborhood Services (CNS) is requesting  authorization for the 
Mayor  to execute an environmental review for the acquisition and maintenance of McKenzie I & II 
Court  Apartments by Tuscaloosa Housing Authority (THA).
 
 
Resolution Summary:  
 CNS requests a uthorization  for the Mayor to execute an environmental review for the acquisition and 
maintenance of McKenzie I & II Court Apartments  by Tuscaloosa Housing Authority (THA) that is 
categorically excluded per 24 CFR 58.35 (a), and subject to laws and authorities at Section 58.5 to support the City’s review of the completed environmental assessment. 
 
Funding:  
 None  
 Committee Action:  
 CNS requests a uthorization for the Mayor to execute an environmental review for the acquisition 
and maintenance of McKenzie I & II Court  Apartments  by Tuscaloosa Housing Authority (THA) .
 
 
3

Memorandum  
 
To: Public Projects Committee  
 From:  Bryan Gurney, PE  
Water & Sewer Department  
 Date:  April 14, 2026  
 Re: Authorization of Professional Services Agreement with Thornton, Musso, Bellemin, Inc.  
for Evaluation of Filter Media at Ed Love  
   
 
 
Request:  
Requesting authorization of professional services contract with Thornton, Musso, Bellemin , Inc. 
in the amount of $17,127.20 for the sampling and analysis of our filter media at Ed Love WTP.  
 Project Description:  
Ed Love has several water filters that contain different layers of media to remove particles from 
water before the water moves on to other treatment steps. Over time, the media loses its  
functionality and needs replacing. With this evaluation, TMB will analyze  the media to 
determine if it needs cleaning or replacing.   
 Project Schedule:  
This project will take approximately 6 weeks to complete.  
 Project Funding:  
Funded by Ed Love 60109045 -3100. 
6
 
 
 
 
Memorandum  
 
To: Public Projects Committee  
 From:  Bryan Gurney, PE  
Water & Sewer Department  
 Date:  April 17, 2026  
 Re: Authorization of Professional Services Agreement with Thornton,  
Musso, & Bellemin, Inc.  
   
 
 
Request:  
Requesting authorization for a professional services contract with Thornton, Musso, &  Bellemin, 
Inc. for an intermediate wastewater treatment  program at Lift Stations 2 and 33.  Total contract 
is $22,445.50.  
 Project Description:  
This will be a ﬁeld study where specialized treatment is applied in a deﬁned area of the collection system and its performance will be evaluated. Thornton, Musso, & Bellemin  will 
oversee the application and performance tracking.  
 Project Schedule:  
This contract will be for a period of 40 days.  
 Project Funding:  
WRRF Outside Services 60109041 -3100.  
7
 
 
 
 
Memorandum  
 
To: Public Projects Committee  
 From:  Kimberly Michael, PE  
Water & Sewer Department  
 Date:  April 17, 2026  
 Re: 2025 Municipal Water Pollution Prevention Report Summary for Hilliard N Fletcher 
Water Resource Recovery Facility  
 
  General Information  
 The Municipal Water Pollution Prevention Report (MWPP) is a required annual submission to ADEM. The MWPP summarizes the condition of the entire sanitary sewer system during the prior calendar year and is due annually by May 31st.  Each sanitary sewer system permitted by ADEM receives a point total between 0 and 783 points.  Lower point totals are preferable.   
 The Hilliard N Fletcher WRRF received a total score of 1 40 points for calendar year 202 5. 
8
